the diagram models a system with classes representing users, posts, comments, business users, chat lists, and neighborhoods.
the relationships show how these classes are connected, such as a post being associated with a user and a user being associated with a chat list.
businessuser inherits from user, indicating a specialization of the user class.
the diagram focuses on the structure of the data model rather than any specific processes or interactions.
start -> usercreateprocedure — type=transition; label=""; explanation="start the user creation process"
usercreateprocedure -> utente creato? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="check if the user has been created"
utente creato? -> authenticationprocedure — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="if the user is not created, proceed with authentication"
utente creato? -> verifylocalizationprocedure — type=transition; label="si"; explanation="if the user is created, proceed with localization verification"
authenticationprocedure -> credenziali valide per l'autorizzazione? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="check if credentials are valid for authorization"
credenziali valide per l'autorizzazione? -> home — type=transition; label="si"; explanation="if credentials are valid, go to the home page"
credenziali valide per l'autorizzazione? -> authenticationprocedure — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="if credentials are not valid, repeat authentication"
verifylocalizationprocedure -> procedura completata con successo? — type=transition; label=""; explanation="check if the localization procedure has been completed successfully"
procedura completata con successo? -> home — type=transition; label="si"; explanation="if the procedure is completed successfully, go to the home page"
procedura completata con successo? -> verifylocalizationprocedure — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="if the procedure is not completed successfully, repeat localization verification"
flow 1: start -> usercreateprocedure -> utente creato? (no) -> authenticationprocedure -> credenziali valide per l'autorizzazione? (si) -> home
flow 2: start -> usercreateprocedure -> utente creato? (si) -> verifylocalizationprocedure -> procedura completata con successo? (si) -> home
the diagram models the process of user creation and authentication within a system.
the process begins with user creation, followed by authentication if the user wasn't created.
if the user is created, the process proceeds to localization verification.
both authentication and localization verification have loops to handle unsuccessful attempts.
the final destination is the home page, reached after successful authentication or localization.

the diagram models the user interaction flow for viewing and commenting on a post within a general dashboard.
the flow starts at the generaldashboard, navigates to a section, then to a specific post.
after viewing the post, the user can add a comment, which is then saved, returning to the full screen post view.
there are no explicit error handling or alternative paths shown in the diagram.

main -> storeddatadb — type=message; label="authorization"; explanation="main requests authorization from storeddatadb"
storeddatadb -> main — type=message; label="valid credentials"; explanation="storeddatadb returns valid credentials to main"
main -> usercreateprocedure — type=message; label="authentication"; explanation="main requests authentication from usercreateprocedure"

flow 1: main -> storeddatadb (authorization) -> main (valid credentials) -> usercreateprocedure (authentication) -> storeddatadb (not exist) -> usercreateprocedure (create new entry)
the diagram models the sequence of events involved in creating a new user.
the process begins with main requesting authorization from storeddatadb.
if the credentials are valid, main proceeds to authenticate the user through usercreateprocedure.
usercreateprocedure checks if the user exists in storeddatadb.
if the user does not exist, a new entry is created in storeddatadb.
